# Backward Compatibility Checklist: Retro-migration - Version Marker and State-Keyed Backfill/Relocate

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of PRSG-011 backward-compatibility requirements for legacy exemption, marker-gated navigation backfill, in-flight spec skipping, out-of-scope namespace handling, and compatibility with existing MOC lints and `generate-spec-index.sh`.
**Created**: 2026-06-09
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Legacy Gate and MOC Lint Compatibility

- [x] CHK001 - Does the spec preserve the existing "no marker means exempt" contract for legacy specs until an operator explicitly thaws and migrates them? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions; PRSG-002 §FR-013, §FR-023; PRSG-003 §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK002 - Is the repo-level `.specify/structure-version.json` marker scoped to roadmap-MOC legacy-row discovery, without turning every historical spec into a per-spec version-gated MOC? [Clarity, Spec §FR-023; Data Model §RepositoryStructureMarker, §SpecMocMarker]
- [x] CHK003 - Are absent, unreadable, malformed, non-integer, or below-v1 repo markers specified so `generate-spec-index.sh` preserves pre-marker behavior and skips legacy or non-version-marked specs? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-023, Edge Cases; Data Model §RepositoryStructureMarker]
- [x] CHK004 - Do requirements stay compatible with the existing orphan and stale-index lint invariant that gated MOC checks fire only for `SPEC-MOC.md` files carrying a bare integer `structureVersion >= 1`? [Consistency, Existing lints `validate-moc-orphan.sh`, `validate-moc-stale-index.sh`; Spec §Assumptions]

## Tier-0 Historical Navigation Backfill

- [x] CHK005 - Does Tier-0 make completed or archived historical specs navigable through durable generated rows rather than by stamping, moving, or renaming historical files? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, §FR-008, §FR-023]
- [x] CHK006 - Are the durable Tier-0 targets ordered and explicit for version-gated MOCs, legacy on-disk specs without gated MOCs, and archive-memory-only specs? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007; Contract §Tier-0 Rules; Data Model §SpecCandidate]
- [x] CHK007 - Is Tier-0 delegated to `generate-spec-index.sh` or the existing generated-zone writer, with `migrate-structure.sh` prohibited from maintaining a second INDEX renderer or direct INDEX patcher? [Consistency, Spec §FR-023, §FR-024; Plan §Increment 1; Research §Decision 1]
- [x] CHK008 - Does the spec keep per-spec SPEC-MOC generated zones unchanged while applying legacy-row discovery only to roadmap-MOC INDEX rows? [Backward Compatibility, Spec §FR-023; PRSG-004 §FR-018]

## Active Feature and In-Flight Spec Skips

- [x] CHK009 - Is `.specify/feature.json` the only authoritative source for frozen/in-flight migration state, with branch name inference explicitly excluded? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, §Assumptions; Data Model §ActiveFeatureState]
- [x] CHK010 - Is active-feature parsing tri-state and complete enough to distinguish absent, valid frozen target, and invalid states? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009; Data Model §ActiveFeatureState; Contract §Active-Feature Rules]
- [x] CHK011 - Are valid in-flight specs skipped in every migration tier and reported with a frozen/in-flight reason? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009, §FR-023; Contract §Tier-0 Rules; Relocation Contract §Apply Behavior]
- [x] CHK012 - Do invalid active-feature states block every mutating mode before backup or mutation while remaining reportable in dry-run? [Operator Safety, Spec §FR-009; Contract §Active-Feature Rules]

## Tier-2 Relocation Backward Compatibility

- [x] CHK013 - Is Tier-2 relocation an explicit codemod only, with scaffold and autopilot flows limited to static suggestions and prohibited from executing dry-run or apply? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019, §FR-020, §FR-026; Research §Decision 6]
- [x] CHK014 - Are CONTRACT artifacts protected from relocation even when filenames resemble PROCESS allow-list items? [Backward Compatibility, Spec §FR-014; Relocation Contract §CONTRACT Protections]
- [x] CHK015 - Are docs-side PROCESS relocations limited to thawed spec ID-matching design/workflow files, leaving unrelated docs, roadmaps, PRDs, and already-current `.process/` files untouched? [Coverage, Spec §FR-025; Relocation Contract §Dual PROCESS Anchors]
- [x] CHK016 - Are successful Tier-1/Tier-0 and Tier-2 reruns specified as no-op states with no backup, file write, move, stamp, or generated-zone update when no mutation is pending? [Measurability, Spec §FR-006, §FR-018, §SC-007]

## Out-of-Scope Legacy Namespaces

- [x] CHK017 - Are deterministic criteria defined for classifying non-SpecKit and date-named legacy namespaces as `skipped_out_of_scope`, so they cannot be accidentally included in Tier-0 navigation rows, Tier-2 relocation candidates, or scaffold/autopilot suggestions? [Resolved, Completeness, Spec §FR-027; Data Model §SpecCandidate; Report Contract §items[].reason; Migration/Relocation Contracts §Candidate Eligibility]
- [x] CHK018 - Does the JSON report vocabulary include an explicit out-of-scope skip action so excluded candidates can be reported without being treated as missing, invalid, or frozen? [Traceability, Spec §FR-021; Report Contract §items[].action; Data Model §SpecCandidate]

## Verification Coverage

- [x] CHK019 - Does the plan call for fixtures that preserve marker-absent/malformed behavior, frozen/in-flight skip behavior, archive-memory target selection, and ID normalization false-join protection? [Coverage, Plan §Increment 1; Quickstart §Focused Verification]
- [x] CHK020 - Do success criteria measure no-regression outcomes for dry-run read-only behavior, dirty-tree mutation blocks, no-stamp/no-move Tier-0 backfill, and idempotent reruns? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, §SC-002, §SC-003, §SC-007]

## Rerun Verification

- [x] CHK021 - Is candidate eligibility now defined before Tier-0 row rendering, Tier-2 move discovery, and scaffold/autopilot suggestion emission? [Spec §FR-027; Research §Decision 8]
- [x] CHK022 - Are stable out-of-scope reasons present in the data model and shared JSON report contract? [Data Model §SpecCandidate; Report Contract §items[].reason]
- [x] CHK023 - Do plan and quickstart verification requirements now cover no Tier-0 rows, no Tier-2 move candidates, and no scaffold/autopilot suggestions for out-of-scope namespaces? [Plan §Implementation Increments; Quickstart §Focused Verification]

## Notes

- Initial backward-compatibility run found one gap: out-of-scope non-SpecKit/date-named namespace handling was mentioned as scope, and the report contract included `skipped_out_of_scope`, but deterministic candidate criteria were not explicit enough across the spec artifacts.
- CHK017 was resolved by adding a pre-normalization candidate eligibility predicate, stable `non_speckit_namespace` and `date_named_legacy_namespace` reasons, Tier-0/Tier-2 no-row/no-move behavior, and no-suggestion coverage across `spec.md`, `data-model.md`, the CLI/report contracts, `plan.md`, `quickstart.md`, and `research.md`.
- Rerun verification added CHK021-CHK023 and found no new gaps.
